title: spyridium scabridum
intro: Spyridium scabridum, also known as the scabrid spyridium or rough spyridium, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Celastraceae. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Spyridium scabridum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin health, as well as its possible role in supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically harvested, dried, and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
